stationDescription: Velocity-area station, no cableway, thin-plate weir control. Reservoir storage (Stoneyford) in catchment with abstractions for public water supply - minor net effect.
catchmentDescription: Geology: mainly basalt overlain with till. Catchment is largely upland, predominantly grassland (significant area of rough grassland) with shrub heath and bare rock outcrops. No significant urban development.
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Braid at Ballee
stationDescription: Velocity-area station with cableway. Flows from 1980 onwards reprocessed in 2002. Two small impounding reservoirs (capacity 409 Ml combined) for a public water extraction of 5 Ml/d. Town effluent returned to river; heavy weed growth in river at Ballee due to effluent discharges. Flows gauged to 51.5m3/s (around bankfull).
catchmentDescription: Geology entirely Upper and Lower Basalt extensively exposed with thin covering of till. Some alluvium, sand and gravel near to the river. Predominantly improved grassland with some coniferous woodland, rising to 400m; some intensive pig and poultry units. Ballymena is the major settlement (pop. 28,700).
operator: Rivers Agency
farText: E: Runoff increased by effluent returns.

name: Agivey at Whitehill
stationDescription: Velocity-area station with cableway. Rating considered stable. Flows from 1975 onwards reprocessed in 2002.
catchmentDescription: Geology: mainly basalt overlain by till with some peat. Significant proportion of upland, predominantly grassland with significant bog and some shrub heath and coniferous woodland. No urban areas or major industry.
operator: Rivers Agency
farText: N: Natural to within 10% at the 95 percentile flow.

name: Six-Mile Water at Ballyclare
stationDescription: Velocity-area station without cableway (closed 1990). Headwaters contain Dungonell reservoir.
catchmentDescription: Catchment is almost entirely basalt with considerable superficial deposits of till. Except for the  the catchment is predominantly improved grassland.  Some urban/suburban development - includes the small market town of Ballyclare (pop. 7,800).
operator: Rivers Agency
farText: N: Natural to within 10% at the 95 percentile flow.

name: Upper Bann at Bannfield
stationDescription: Velocity-area station with cableway and Flat V control (installed in 1989), natural control previously. Flows for full POR reprocessed in 2002. Rating is derived from current meter gaugings. Simple extrapolation occurs above approx 46m3/s. More high flow gaugings would be useful. Period of missing flows between January and March 2009 caused by instrument failure. Flows for late December 2010 considered suspect due to likely freezing in stilling well. Reservoir storage (Spelga and Lough Island Reavy) in catchment with abstractions for PWS the net effect of which is minor. The station is used to monitor a prescribed flow of 18 Ml/d.
catchmentDescription: The Upper Bann drains the Mourne Mountains. Geology: impermeable (granite and quartzite) overlain with substantial amounts of superficial deposits (till). The catchment is predominantly grassland with some shrub heath; no significant urban fraction.
operator: Rivers Agency
farText: R: Regulation from surface water and/or ground water.

name: Rocky at Rocky Mountain
stationDescription: Flat V weir, approx. 6.1 metres wide in steep mountain stream - pebble/cobble bed, large boulders may settle in measuring reach during floods. Theoretical calibration - some confirmatory gaugings (by wading) completed. All but notable floods contained. Flows for full POR reprocessed in 2002; pre-1986 flows removed from NRFA. Natural and responsive regime. Catchment rainfall may be underestimated.
catchmentDescription: A rugged, impervious catchment - with some thin peat cover - in the Mourne Mountains. 51% shrub heath and 49% rough grass cover (both maxima for NI gauged catchments).
operator: Rivers Agency
farText: N: Natural to within 10% at the 95 percentile flow.

name: Clogh at Tullynewey
stationDescription: Velocity-area station with cableway. Headwaters contain Dungonnell reservoir, yielding some 12.5 Ml/d. Out of bank at flows below QMED. Rating derived from current meter gaugings from cableway. Simple extrapolation occurs beyond 35m3/s. High flow gaugings carried out in October 2002 have confirmed rating. Bed stability issues affect the accuracy of low flows; June/July 2009 low flows thought to be underestimated and deemed unreliable. Flows for full period of record reprocessed in 2002.
catchmentDescription: Geology is Lower Basalt stratum with drift deposits, mainly till in upper areas with alluvium an sand and gravels in lower valleys. Agricultural area, sparsely populated - predominantly grassland with some shrub heath.
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Lower Bann at Movanagher
stationDescription: Velocity-area station, no cableway, control is masonry weir 800m d/s (built for angling). Flows for full POR reprocessed in 2002. Very artificial flow pattern - regulated by sluices u/s at Portna and Toome. Station measures outflow from 37% of NI. Lough Neagh (385 sq.km) is within catchment, containing 3636 Mm3 of water. Total net export of water from catchment is approx. 200 Ml/d. Some low flows between 2011 and 2013 affected by refurbishment works at Portna sluices (e.g. March 2012 & May 2013).
catchmentDescription: Catchment contains all solid and drift deposits present in N.Ireland. Numerous minor aquifers developed for PWS. Catchment is agricultural - predominantly improved grassland but with pop. approx. 50,000 concentrated in 8 urban centres.
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Crumlin at Cidercourt Bridge
stationDescription: Velocity-area station with cableway and natural control. Flows from 1983 onwards reprocessed in 2002. High flows gauged up to 34 cumecs above which rating is extrapolated. August 2008 peak flows above bankfull and subject to considerable uncertainty. 2009 low flows considered unreliable and are under review.
catchmentDescription: Catchment geology is impermeable (mainly basalt) overlain by till. Mostly upland - predominantly improved grassland. No major urban areas, but contains Crumlin (pop. 2,700), some scattered light industry.
operator: Rivers Agency
farText: N: Natural to within 10% at the 95 percentile flow.

name: Oonawater at Shanmoy
stationDescription: Velocity-area station with cableway; Flat V weir constructed in 1986. Flows for full period of record reprocessed in 2002. Rating is derived from current meter gaugings. Simple extrapolation occurs above 34.71m3/s (highest gauged level). More high flow ratings are required. Late December 2010 flows considered suspect due to likely freezing in stilling well.
catchmentDescription: Geology predominantly Carboniferous Limestone with some Devonian Conglomerate overlain by till. Predominantly improved grassland with no significant centres of habitation. Highest total grass cover (92%) of gauged NI catchments.
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Rathmore Burn at Rathmore Bridge
stationDescription: Velocity-area station. Flows for full period of record reprocessed in 2002. Late December 2010 flows considered suspect due to likely freezing in stilling well.
catchmentDescription: Predominantly improved grassland, but rock outcrops also; some horticulture (mainly potatoes). No significant urban centres.
operator: Rivers Agency
farText: N: Natural to within 10% at the 95 percentile flow.

name: Clady at Clady Bridge
stationDescription: Velocity-area station. Flows for full period of record reprocessed in 2002. The rating is derived from current meter gaugings. Simple extrapolation occurs beyond approx 14m3/s. More high flow gaugings would be useful.
catchmentDescription: Geology predominantly basal overlain by glacial till. Landuse mainly improved grassland with no significant urban development.
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Ballysally Blagh at University of Ulster
stationDescription: V-notch weir. Flows for full POR reprocessed in 2002. Rating extrapolated above around 0.8m (2 cumecs). Flows between Sept 2010 and July 2011 altered to take account of suspected debris on weir crest and should be treated with caution.
catchmentDescription: Flows from low hills across agricultural land and then through suburban area. Soils outside the urban area are water gleys.
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Main at Dunminning
stationDescription: Velocity-area station with cableway located immediately d/s of a radial-gated flood control structure. Flows for full POR reprocessed in 2002. Instrument failure lead to missing data in August 2010. U/s reservoir effectively transfers 12 Ml/d to points d/s of the station.
catchmentDescription: Geology predominantly basalt overlain by till with alluvium in valleys. Landuse mainly improved grassland but catchment contains Glarryford bog.
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Main at Shane's Viaduct
stationDescription: Velocity-area station with cableway and natural control. Flows for full period of record reprocessed in 2002. The rating is derived from current meter gaugings. Simple extrapolation occurs above approx 226 m3/s. Gaugings cover wide range and rating is good. Periods of missing flow in 2010 (July-November) and 2011 (May-June) due to instrument failure. Net effect of abstractions and returns is minor. Major arterial drainage scheme, channel deepening and bridge replacement, was implemented in the 1980s.
catchmentDescription: Geology almost entirely basalt overlain by till (covering over 50% of the catchment) with alluvium and sands and gravels along valleys. Significant upland areas, predominantly improved grassland. Extensive bogland in the north. Contains Ballymena (pop. 28,704, 2001 census, - substantial light industry), and Randalstown (pop. 4,944, 2001 census).
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Upper Bann at Moyallen
stationDescription: Velocity-area station with cableway and natural control. Flows for full period of record reprocessed in 2002. Replaced 203017 in 1990.
catchmentDescription: Predominantly improved grassland, some horticulture (mainly potatoes). Urban areas: Banbridge (pop. 12,500) and Rathfriland (2,100).
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Bush at Seneirl Bridge
stationDescription: Velocity-area station with cableway and natural control; some weedgrowth problems in the measuring reach. Well gauged to QMED; some out-of-bank flow above. Full period of record reprocessed in 2002 (but existing data retained for 1974 and Oct/Nov 1973). The rating is derived from current meter gaugings. Simple extrapolation occurs above approx 63m3/s (highest gauged level). Re-calibrated at high end recently. Altnahinch Reservoir at the head of the river has a minor impact.
catchmentDescription: Geology predominantly basalts with a major fault bisecting the catchment. Some schists in upper reaches with a little greensand. Extensive Drift cover, peat on high ground. Predominantly grassland with some horticulture (mainly potatoes), coniferous woodland and shrub heath. No significant urban centres apart from Ballymoney (pop. 9,009, 2001 census).
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Lagan at Dunmurry
stationDescription: Velocity-area station with cableway. Numerous PWS boreholes in Sherwood S'st, pumping capacity almost 30 Ml/d. All effluent is returned to the river. Unstable bank, silt build-up, and difficulty in gauging contributed to station closure in 1985.
catchmentDescription: Geology: 60% Silurian; remainder is Sherwood S'st with some breccia, Chalk, Hibernian Greensand and Lower Basalts. Heavily overlain with till and extensive sand and gravel deposits in lower reaches of the river. Mainly arable, some upland heath. Urbanised in Lisburn and SW Belfast.
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Lagan at Newforge
stationDescription: Velocity-area station with cableway. Flows from start of 1980 onwards reprocessed in 2002. Gets out of bank on RH bank, some bypassing on LH bank but this is not measured. The rating is derived from current meter gaugings from cableway. Simple extrapolation occurs above approx 128 m3/s. Nov. 2000 peak comparable with 1978 maxima. Numerous PWS boreholes - pumping capacity total nearly 30 Ml/d. All effluent returns to the river.
catchmentDescription: Geology is 60% Silurian sedimentary rocks; remainder - Sherwood Sandstone with some breccia, Chalk, Hibernian Greensand and Lower Basalts. Overlain with till; substantial sand and gravel deposits in lower valley. Predominantly improved grassland, some horticulture (mainly potatoes). Contains dense urban areas of south Belfast, Lisburn also (pop. 71,403, 2001 census).
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Ravernet at Ravernet
stationDescription: Flat V weir installed autumn 1977, width 8.64m. Height of wing walls 2.1m. Theoretical rating applies up to bankfull; exceedence very unlikely. High flow rating from current meter gaugings; simple extrapolation above 19.4 m3/s. Previously a VA station - flow data of lesser accuracy, data removed in 2002. Flows from start of 1980 reprocessed in 2002. Natural flow regime; significant storage in headwater loughs.
catchmentDescription: Geology mainly Silurian sedimentary rocks overlain by till. Predominantly improved grassland with some horticulture (mainly potatoes). No significant urban development.
operator: Rivers Agency
farText: N: Natural to within 10% at the 95 percentile flow.

name: Lagan at Drumiller
stationDescription: Velocity-area station with calibration by wading. Flat V weir installed in 1991, leading to missing period of data. Gauged to approx. 1.2 QMED. Minor floodplain flow (left bank) during floods. High flow gauging from bridge on d/s side, as u/s flows less uniform. Simple extrapolation used above max gauging of 30.77m3/s. Flows not contained. Prior to weir installation rating based solely on gaugings. Flows from 1978 onwards reprocessed in 2002. Late December 2010 flows considered suspect due to likely freezing in stilling well. Natural regime - no water abstractions or significant effluent returns.
catchmentDescription: Geology: entirely Silurian overlain with limited till. Catchment rises to over 500m. Predominantly improved grassland and upland heath. Catchment contains one large village (Dromora, pop. 597, 2001 census).
operator: Rivers Agency
farText: N: Natural to within 10% at the 95 percentile flow.

name: Lagan at Banoge
stationDescription: Velocity-area station, once with cableway, but now calibrated by wading. Subject to weed problems at low summer flows and goes out of bank at high flows; station closed in 1994. Flows from start of 1985 onwards reprocessed in 2002. No abstractions, Dromore effluent returns to river.
catchmentDescription: Geology: entirely Silurian overlain with till. 35% upland heath rising to over 500 mOD; remainder agricultural grassland except for the town of Dromore (pop. 3,000).
operator: Rivers Agency
farText: N: Natural to within 10% at the 95 percentile flow.

name: Annacloy at Kilmore Bridge
stationDescription: Velocity-area station in straight reach (bridge, with piers, 60m upstream). Weed growth can affect low flow levels and bank vegetation can affect high flows. Goes out of bank on RH bank but contained up to more than QMED. Flows for full period of record reprocessed in 2002. Responsive regime. High flow rating derived from current meter gaugings (cableway); low flows gauged by wading, u/s at the bridge. Simple extrapolation beyond 60.4m3/s (max gauging). No significant artificial influences.
catchmentDescription: Silurain solid geology with scattered Tertiary and Caledonian intrusions, overlain by drumlin terrain. Predominantly improved grassland, some urban development (including Ballynahinch, pop. 5,334, 2001 census).
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Cotton at Grandmere
stationDescription: Velocity-area station. Simple extrapolation used above maximum gauging of 5.64m3/s. Flows reprocessed in 2002. Missing flows in Oct/Nov 2010 due to instrument failure.
catchmentDescription: Geology is Silurain sedimentary rocks overlain by a limited coverage of peat and alluvium. Mostly flat grassland with some urban development (Bangor). Soil is shallow brown earth with organic alluvium & surface water gleys.
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Enler at Comber
stationDescription: Flat V weir in trapezoidal channel containing the full range of flows. Rating revised following high flow gaugings in 2000/01 (up to 23.6 cumecs), all flows reprocessed (producing substantial reduction in highest flows). High flow rating from current meter gaugings from bridge downstream; simple extrapolation beyond 1.443m (max gauging) Low flows gauged by wading downstream of weir. A limited number of boreholes nearby exist with pumped extraction to water supply. Station vandalised during August 2008 leading to period of missing data.
catchmentDescription: Geology predominantly Silurian sandstones with Permian and Triassic sandstones together with limited coverage of Basalt and Dolerite. This is overlain by glacial sands and gravels along the main river valley. Predominantly grassland but highest proportion (26%) of horticulture (mainly potatoes) for any gauged NI catchment. Some urban/suburban development in the upper reaches, Dundonald.
operator: Rivers Agency
farText: N: Natural to within 10% at the 95 percentile flow.

name: Lagan at Feeny
stationDescription: Velocity-area station with cableway and natural control. Replaced 205011. Simple rating extrapolation used above 35m3/s. Flows for full period of record reprocessed in 2002.
catchmentDescription: Predominantly improved grassland, some horticulture (mainly potatoes).
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Woodburn at Control
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Blackstaff at Eason's
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Knock at Orangefield
stationDescription: Velocity-area station. Flows reprocessed in 2002.
catchmentDescription: Small urban catchment east of Belfast, (approx. 50% urban). Main stream rises 180m to hills on NE. Disturbed soils in urban area, free draining soils elsewhere
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Clanrye at Mountmill Bridge
stationDescription: Velocity-area station calibrated by wading and from footbridge. Flows contained apart from extreme events. Gaugings up to 1.2QMED, more needed to define flood range. Flows reprocessed in 2002. Channel works in early 1970's affected station, requiring separate calibration. Pre-1975 data removed from the NRFA (including spurious extreme flow in Jan. 1973) due to its poor quality. Natural regime.
catchmentDescription: Entirely Old Red Sandstone; some glacial till, peat and limited river alluvium also. Predominantly improved grassland with some horticulture (mainly potatoes).
operator: Rivers Agency
farText: N: Natural to within 10% at the 95 percentile flow.

name: Jerretspass at Jerretspass
stationDescription: Velocity-area station calibrated by wading and from footbridge. Flows from start of 1981 onwards reprocessed in 2002. Low catchment runoff - effective catchment area for flows below the mean is 34.8 sq km - a canal intercepts drainage from 73 km from the west (during high flows spillage occurs to the Jerrettspass).
catchmentDescription: Geology: Llandovery in age. Occasional igneous intrusions. Catchment is entirely rural: predominantly improved grassland with some horticulture (mainly potatoes).
operator: Rivers Agency
farText: N: Natural to within 10% at the 95 percentile flow.

name: Bessbrook at Carnbane
stationDescription: Velocity area station located at road bridge. Channel width approx. 5m. Weed growth problematic at low flows. Significant bridge works in 1999 thought to have had a marked affect on the rating. Station not currently considered suitable for inclusion on NRFA, pending further review of rating.
operator: Rivers Agency

name: Annalong at Recorder
operator: Belfast City & District Water Commissioners

name: Colebrooke at Ballindarragh Bridge
stationDescription: Velocity-area station with cableway and natural control. Flows from start of 1987 reprocessed in 2002. Low flow gauging problem - difficult to wade as it is deep, and low velocities. The rating is derived from current meter gaugings. Simple extrapolation occurs beyond 129.6m3/s. Not contained at QMED.
catchmentDescription: Geology has Old Red Sandstone in headwaters with Carboniferous formations (l'st and s'sts) in lower catchment.  Overlain by glacial deposits; sand and gravels along river valleys. Land use is predominantly grassland with significant coniferous woodland, some bog and no significant urban development.
operator: Rivers Agency
farText: N: Natural to within 10% at the 95 percentile flow.

name: Sillees at Drumrainey Bridge
stationDescription: Velocity-area station with cableway. Throttling occurs at extreme high flows (>3.25m stage, c.30 cumecs) at downstream bridge soffit. Flows from start of 1984 onwards reprocessed in 2002. Some natural storage provided by small lakes in headwaters. The rating is derived from current meter gaugings. Simple extrapolation occurs beyond 27.6m3/s (highest gauged level). Good range of high flow gaugings. Not gauged over full range of low flows at site.
catchmentDescription: Geology mainly Carboniferous formations (mostly l'st and shales). Predominantly grassland with significant coniferous woodland. No significant urban development.
operator: Rivers Agency
farText: E: Runoff increased by effluent returns.

name: Ballinamallard at Ballycassidy
stationDescription: Velocity-area station with cableway and natural control. All flows reprocessed in 2002.
catchmentDescription: Predominantly improved grassland.
operator: Rivers Agency
